page_subtitle: UN Sustainable Development Goal 3: Ensure healthy lives and promote well-being for all at all ages.
progress_note: Maternal mortality fell to 197 per 100,000 (2023) — target is <70 by 2030.
voice_hint: 💡 Say "Tell me about maternal health" to surface any card by voice.
disclaimer_card: This information is educational. Consult a qualified health professional for personal medical advice.

cards.title: Maternal Health (SDG 3.1)
cards.body: Regular antenatal care, skilled birth attendance, and postnatal check-ups save lives. Seek care early in pregnancy.

cards.title: Child Health (SDG 3.2)
cards.body: Exclusive breastfeeding for 6 months, timely vaccinations, and ORS for diarrhoea prevent most under-5 deaths.

cards.title: Infectious Diseases (SDG 3.3)
cards.body: HIV, TB, malaria, and hepatitis remain major threats. Know your status, use protection, complete treatment courses.

cards.title: Non-Communicable Diseases (SDG 3.4)
cards.body: Heart disease, diabetes, and cancer cause 74% of global deaths. Regular screening, a balanced diet, and daily movement matter.

cards.title: Mental Health (SDG 3.4)
cards.body: 1 in 8 people worldwide live with a mental disorder. Talk to someone you trust. Help is available — you are not alone.

cards.title: Substance Use (SDG 3.5)
cards.body: Tobacco, alcohol, and drugs cause millions of preventable deaths. Counselling and support services can help you quit.

cards.title: Universal Health Coverage (SDG 3.8)
cards.body: Everyone deserves quality health services without financial hardship. Know your rights to free or subsidised care.

cards.title: Vaccines & Medicines (SDG 3.b)
cards.body: Vaccines prevent 3.5–5M deaths yearly. Follow your national immunisation schedule and keep a vaccination record.
